learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate mastery and enhanced knowledge in the chosen research field
Produce a new body of knowledge and/or improve an existing body of knowledge in a chosen research field
Demonstrate enhanced knowledge and skills in data collection, synthesis and analysis
Demonstrate the ability to test, validate and evaluate research results
Demonstrate enhanced skills in critical thinking and logical analysis
Exhibit improved management skills to complete quality research tasks in a timely manner
Communicate effectively with expert and non-expert audiences through written, oral and graphical skills

assessments.due_date: Submission of deliverables and progress report 24/08/2026 4:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale

Presentation and discussion of developed Research Proposal. Submission electronically by 2pm. In person presentation to the seminar from 2-5 pm. This assessment is marked on the written submission only. This assessment item can draw on previously submissions in GEOS6001 but is required to show development consistent with five weeks of work in PLAN4009. The proposal should include the following items:

  • the background to the intended research;
  • the research question, aims and objectives; the research methods;
  • the expected outcomes;
  • the significance of the research and its contribution to new knowledge;
  • the budget for the research (if applicable, 2000 w)

Formative Feedback: You should be meeting and discussing drafts regularly with your advisor(s) during teaching weeks. Formative feedback is provided prior to submission. Following the assessment of the documentation, students will be required to make any recommended corrections or amendments to the satisfaction of the supervisor and course coordinator.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and/or MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: The late penalty for this assessment item will be calculated as follows:
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 2 – Thesis Development and Final Document
assessments.due_date:
Thesis development and final document 19/10/2026 4:00 pm

Oral Presentation 20/10/2026 9:00 am
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale

This assessment requires students to demonstrate the application of their approved research methodology and to present findings from their thesis project. Students are expected to show evidence of substantive research activity, including data collection, analysis, and/or data-informed design exploration. 

The assessment marks the transition from research design to research execution. Students must demonstrate the implementation of their methodological approach as proposed, or appropriately adapted in response to findings, and that initial conclusions or insights have emerged.

The expected word limit for the final planning thesis is 8,000-12,000 words. The requirements for the Thesis are a demonstration of research design, methodology and argument as in the Research Proposal developed by the student and agreed by the supervisor.

Formative Feedback: You should be meeting and discussing drafts regularly with your advisor(s) during teaching weeks. Formative feedback is provided prior to submission. Following the assessment of the documentation, students will be required to make any recommended corrections or amendments to the satisfaction of the supervisor and course coordinator.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(27 Jul - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Supervision

Regular meetings with your advisor, to the equivalent of one hour per week. Regular attendance and showing work in progress is a requirement. Progress to be recorded in the progress report document.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
